_meta.author: scorecard-refine-local autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize Fort Worth, TX City Council/Mayor batch (Parker, Beck, Hill/Macy, Martinez, Hall/Mia). Local tier (30 cells). Evidence-first: every TRUE/FALSE cites a source URL; null where no public position. Party correction: Beck listed R -> actually a Democrat (Planned Parenthood board, 2020 Dem primary for TX House 97).

mattie-parker.notes_set: Mayor of Fort Worth (nonpartisan office; known Republican, the lone GOP mayor of a major Texas city). Re-elected to a third 2-year term May 3, 2025 (~67%); term ends 2027. United Methodist; maintains a city Faith Cabinet. Evidence-scored 2026-06: HARD pro-police (FWPOA endorsement, 'to be a good mayor you have to be pro-police'); fiscal restraint (FY2026 67c rate, 'largest tax-rate cut in 34 years'); public Christian faith leadership (council invocations, urged faith leaders to 'cover our city in prayer', Faith Cabinet). DEVIATIONS from GOP/RESOLUTE expectations: backed gun control (raise rifle age 18->21 after Uvalde) = self_defense FALSE; issued 2025 Pride 'y'all means all' recognition = biblical_marriage q4 FALSE (counter-note: removed a 'Pride' badge from a city kids' reading program in 2023 citing parental authority). No public position found on abortion, election integrity, immigration (declined comment) -> null. COVID record was deference TO the state (sided with Abbott, voted against defying mask-mandate ban) which does not map to the local-control-vs-state cells -> null.

0.note: No public statement on personhood/abortion found.

1.note: No vote/advocacy on abortion abolition found.

0.note: No statement affirming/denying marriage definition found.

2.note: No statement on transgender ideology found.

4.note: Issued June 2025 city Pride recognition, 'in Fort Worth, y'all means all' embracing 'identities' in city policy. Counter-note: in 2023 removed a 'Pride' badge from a city summer reading program citing parental authority.

0.note: On school choice only soft/inevitability framing ('vouchers will pass'); also pushed public-school funding. No clear endorsement -> null.

1.note: No position on parental consent for minors' medical/gender interventions found.

2.note: 2023 kids'-reading-program badge removal cited parental authority but is a city library program, not K-12 curricula -> not scored here.

0.note: Publicly affirms Christian profession in public office: maintains a city Faith Cabinet, urged faith leaders to 'cover our city in prayer.'

2.note: No position on compelled speech/pronoun mandates found.

3.note: Council opens with invocation+pledge; Oct 2025 organized public prayer at City Hall ('cover our city in prayer').

4.note: No position on state-funded non-Christian displays found.

2.note: Affirmative fiscal restraint: FY2026 rate cut to 67c/$100, described as largest tax-rate cut in 34 years, 5th cut in 6 cycles.

0.note: No position on paper ballots/voting machines found.

1.note: No position on voter ID found.

4.note: Tarrant County took CTCL grants in 2020, but that predates her June 2021 mayoralty and is a county action; no Parker statement found.

0.note: Affirmative pro-police: 'to be a good mayor you have to be pro-police', won Fort Worth Police Officers Association endorsement, publicly pledged strong support for officers.

1.note: No position on cashless bail/rogue-prosecutor policy found.

2.note: No position on victim restitution/proportional punishment found.

3.note: No statement treating/refusing crime as 'public health' found.

4.note: No statement on the magistrate's sword found.

0.note: COVID record was deference TO state authority (sided with Abbott), not assertion of local control against the state -> does not map.

1.note: No evidence of declining state strings.

2.note: Did not impose local mask mandate, but framed as state compliance not local protection from state overreach -> null.

4.note: No refusal to enforce a state directive found.

1.note: Declined to comment on ICE partnership (Aug 2025); no deportation position found.

2.note: No personal position on sanctuary policy found (TX law already bans sanctuary cities).

0.note: No position on constitutional carry found.

1.note: Publicly backed gun control: supported raising the rifle-purchase age from 18 to 21 after Uvalde/Allen, 'we have a crisis.'

profile.confidence_note: Party corrected R->D: Beck won the 2020 Democratic primary for TX House 97 and served on the Planned Parenthood of Greater Texas-Fort Worth community board; press routinely calls her a Fort Worth Democrat/progressive.

elizabeth-m-beck.notes_set: Fort Worth City Council District 9 (downtown/Near Southside); incumbent since June 2021, re-elected May 3, 2025; term ends 2027. PARTY CORRECTION: listed Republican is wrong -- Beck is a Democrat/progressive (2020 Dem primary nominee for TX House 97; served on Planned Parenthood of Greater Texas-Fort Worth community board, endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes). Evidence-scored 2026-06: HARD pro-abortion (PP board + endorsement) -> sanctity_of_life FALSE; HARD pro-LGBTQ (personally championed 2024 Pride certificates of recognition, 'y'all means all') -> biblical_marriage FALSE; defended city DEI programs (2025). No affirmative pro-police backing found (clashed with the police chief over budget) -> public_justice null. No evidence on Christian liberty, election integrity, immigration, or state-overreach cells -> null. A deleted one-word 2A social post is too thin to score.

0.note: Does not affirm personhood-at-conception; pro-choice -- served on Planned Parenthood community board and was endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes for protecting 'safe, legal abortion.'

1.note: Advocates abortion access (PP board/endorsement) -- opposite of abortion abolition.

0.note: Champions LGBTQ inclusion in city policy ('y'all means all'); does not affirm marriage as one man/one woman.

2.note: No specific statement on transgender/biological sex found.

4.note: Personally drafted/championed LGBTQ Pride certificates of recognition after a city Pride proclamation failed (June 2024).

0.note: No position on school choice found.

1.note: No position on parental consent for minors found.

2.note: Defended city DEI programs (2025) but no K-12 CRT/SOGI curricula position -> null.

0.note: No position found.

2.note: Voted to keep tax rate flat (routine) and supported $20 minimum-wage advocates; no deficit/balanced-budget position -> null.

0.note: No position found.
No affirmative pro-police advocacy found; publicly clashed with the police chief over budget/fuel overruns. Does not meet the affirmative-backing bar, and no defund stance -> null.
Defended DEI vs federal anti-DEI pressure (pro-DEI), no state-overreach assertion -> null.
No position found.

1.note: A deleted one-word reply ('Unfortunate') to a 2A comment is too thin to score -> null.

macy-hill.notes_set: Fort Worth City Council District 7 (nonpartisan office; known Republican). Took office May 16, 2023; won re-election unopposed May 3, 2025; term ends 2027. TCU trustee; serves on the Healing Shepherd Clinic (Christian-mission homeless clinic) board -- faith-community engagement, NOT scored as abortion evidence. Evidence-scored 2026-06: one HARD conservative action -- declined to sign the June 2024 city Pride proclamation, blocking it -> biblical_marriage q4 TRUE. Campaign platform explicitly 'Support Police & Fire' -> public_justice q0 TRUE (soft). Otherwise a low public profile: did not respond to press on Pride or on ICE; no documented positions on life, family/schools, Christian liberty, election integrity, state overreach, immigration, or 2A -> null. Note the thin evidence base.

0.note: No abortion statement found (Healing Shepherd board is a homeless clinic, not pro-life work).
No marriage-definition statement found.

4.note: One of four council members who declined to sign the June 2024 city Pride Month proclamation, blocking its passage (fallout ended unanimous proclamations).

0.note: No school-choice position found.
No individual position found (council invocation is a chamber-wide custom).

2.note: Campaign claims tax-rate cuts and voted for flat/cut budgets, but no deficit/balanced-budget position; not a budget dissenter -> null.

0.note: No position found.
Campaign platform affirmatively lists 'Support Police & Fire' / 'Improved Public Safety' as a priority -- a public statement of backing law enforcement (no defund stance).
No position found.

1.note: No position found (not among council members quoted on ICE; did not respond).

0.note: No position found.

profile.confidence_note: Nonpartisan seat; profile is center-left/Democrat-aligned (endorsed by Tarrant County Black Democrats, Sierra Club, labor; staffs Democratic Commissioner Roy Brooks) BUT labor- and police-friendly (Police Officers + Fire Fighters Association endorsements), so 'Moderate/Mixed' is retained as the most honest label.

jeanette-martinez.notes_set: Fort Worth City Council District 11 (city's newest district, created 2022; first Latina on the council). First elected June 2023, won a second term May 3, 2025 (77%); term ends 2027. Center-left/Democrat-aligned but labor- and police-friendly profile. Evidence-scored 2026-06: HARD affirmative pro-police (Fort Worth Police Officers Association endorsement + campaign platform backing 'well-trained and equipped police officers' and community-police partnerships) -> public_justice q0 TRUE. DEVIATIONS from RESOLUTE-expected stance: pro-LGBTQ (supported 2024 Pride certificates; signed solidarity for an LGBTQIA+ advisory subcommittee) -> biblical_marriage q4 FALSE; anti-ICE-cooperation ('if it came down to a vote, I would vote no'; 'very concerned for our Latino community') -> border FALSE. No public position on life, family/schools, Christian liberty, election integrity, or 2A -> null.

0.note: No abortion statement found.
No marriage-definition statement found.

4.note: Supported 2024 LGBTQ Pride certificates of recognition and signed a Sept 2024 solidarity statement backing an LGBTQIA+ advisory subcommittee -- promotion of LGBTQ identity in city policy.

0.note: No school-choice position found.
No position found.

2.note: Voted for flat tax rate and campaigns against tax hikes, but no deficit/balanced-budget position -> null.

0.note: No position found.

Affirmative pro-police: Fort Worth Police Officers Association endorsement plus campaign platform backing 'well-trained and equipped police officers' and community-police partnerships (crime watch, citizens on patrol).
Frames tax burden as a state-legislature failure (soft) -> not scored.

1.note: Opposes ICE/deportation cooperation: 'if it came down to a vote, I would vote no'; 'very concerned for our Latino community.'

2.note: Anti-ICE-partnership / pro-sanctuary-leaning; would vote no on an ICE partnership and aids residents with immigration concerns.

0.note: No position found.

profile.confidence_note: Verified Democrat (ran in the March 2024 Democratic primary for Tarrant County Commissioner Pct 1).

mia-hall.notes_set: Dr. Mia Hall -- Fort Worth City Council District 6 (nonpartisan seat; verified Democrat). Won the June 7, 2025 runoff (57%), took office June 17, 2025; term ends 2027. Career educator (former Crowley ISD board president; FWISD). Evidence-scored 2026-06: HIGH-VALUE catch -- a Democrat with HARD affirmative pro-police backing (Fort Worth Police Officers Association endorsement; campaign backs first responders; Feb 2026 called on FWPD to launch an illegal-gunfire enforcement campaign and citizens-on-patrol partnerships) -> public_justice q0 TRUE. DEVIATION the other way: marched in/celebrated the June 2025 Trinity Pride Festival -> biblical_marriage q4 FALSE. Her youth-gun-violence work is a crime-safety framing, NOT a 2A/gun-control position -> self_defense null. No public position on life, family/schools, Christian liberty, election integrity, state overreach, or immigration -> null.

0.note: No abortion statement found.
No marriage-definition statement found.

4.note: Personally marched in and publicly celebrated the June 2025 Trinity Pride Festival, posting that she was 'honored to celebrate' it.

0.note: Career educator but no council-level school-choice position found.
No position found.

2.note: Part of the 10-1 FY2026 majority that cut the tax rate (fees rose); mainstream, not a balanced-budget/deficit stance -> null.

0.note: No position found.

Affirmative pro-police backing (a Democrat earning the point): Fort Worth Police Officers Association endorsement; campaign backs first responders; Feb 2026 called on FWPD to launch an illegal-gunfire enforcement campaign and urged citizens-on-patrol partnerships.
No position found.
No position found.

1.note: Youth-gun-violence / illegal-gunfire work is a crime-safety framing, not a 2A/gun-control vote -> not scored.
